research.arcananovum.description.activat:
Experience has proven its use for enchanting.
Some old tomes suggest that it can do more.
A specific enchantment that lets it bind to
 physical materials and undo wear and tear.
I haven't discovered any such enchantment
 through use of my enchantment table.
I should try and track down any truth to this.
research.arcananovum.description.advance:
The Overworld has such unique biodiversity!
I must see it all!

These 'Allays' are wonderful! And quite talented!
They also seem to have some innate Arcane abilities.
I've noticed they like certain chimes, but I wonder
 if they like cake.

Old tales mention the sprites of the dark forests.
I've seen dark forests, and they are quite spriteless.
I wonder where they have all gone?

These piercing bolts are quite the trick.
I wonder how many mobs I can kill in one shot?

Every food has such a unique flavor and taste.
I must try them all!

In order to master the natural order,
 I must understand every animal.
To do that I must see the creation of them all.
Surely I can coax animals into reproducing...

If villagers can use a potion stand,
 I should give it a try myself.

My bow skills are a little lacking.
Time to hit the range, and hit the target square on!

Beds have rather explosive consequences in the Nether.
There should be some way to work around this issue.
Cats are so adorable! I want them all!

I wonder if an automated crafting matrix
 is capable of self-replication?

Even this little containment pedestal was enough
 to reveal some of the Nether Star's power!
I wonder to what heights I can push this?
Surely there is something that can block an arrow.

Enderflame sticks and spreads like a liquid.
It is also, obviously, alchemically active.
I should take a sample for further study.

Legends speak of the ancient species that once roamed
 the End with mastery over the skies and stars...
Maybe there are some artifacts of theirs left behind?

I should get a grasp on rudimentary Arcane
 infusion methods before pursuing this.

This Stronghold is an incredible structure!
I wonder who, or what made this stone fortress?
Did they also make the portals? Or is this stronghold
 here to guard or protect the portals?
The libraries here have so much useful knowledge from
 times before the creation of my current history tomes.
I believe the eyes that led me here can open the portal...

I used to think that these eyes always pointed
 in the same direction, but upon the use of
 some precise measuring tools, it looks like they
 point to a particular location far away.
I guess it's time to pack my adventuring bags.

I should get a better appreciation for how
 tall and deep this world goes.
From the highest peak to the bedrock, I
 will experience it all at once!

Most of the intelligent creatures in the Nether
 live in some structures of some sort.
I wonder if there are any that wont attack me.

This is probably a really dumb idea...
But, I really want to drink every potion that
 I can possibly make and see what happens.
These creatures of the night will hunt me no more!

This 'Sculk' is unfathomable! If I had to guess
 it is like some sort of fungus, a giant organism!
To be this dense in experience, it must be absolutely
 ancient, and have a rich supply of nutrients to grow.
How does it get nutrients when its underground?

Lava is such a useful substance.
I should collect some for study.
I wonder how high up this unique effect can take me.

With my new lightning rod, I should put it to use!
I will protect the nearest village from lightning!

Whispers speak of a material stronger than diamond!
It is rumored to be found deep within the Nether...

Some of the old portal ruins I have found
 contain strange obsidian. It has been broken,
 but not physically? It looks like it's crying.
I wonder what possibilities this could unlock.

If I am to fully understand agriculture,
 I must show complete dedication to the craft.
What better way to do that than create a hoe
 from the strongest material there is.

Bows are fine and all, but some villagers
 have told me of their exiled members who
 favor a similar ranged weapon that fires
 slower but with more power.
I should try one of these 'crossbows' out.

This Mace is more powerful than I could've imagined!
It appears to be able to amplify its gravity to a
 near infinite amount by storing my gravitational
 potential energy as it gets converted into speed.
I should see if I can smash something like a 
 zombie with the force of 10 sword hits!

There are some ancient seeds which can only be found
 by a creature of ancient origins, lost to time.
I wish to plant one of these long lost seeds.

With just a few seeds, whole forests grow.
I should learn how to do this myself.

Chiseled bookshelves are quite fashionable.
I wonder if they have any practical use beyond
 the obvious book-storing capabilities.
If skeletons can do it, so can I!
Night time can be scary, perhaps I should sleep it off.

Surely I can outclass a mere skeleton.
I will defeat one from well over 50 blocks!

With so many critters out in the world
 surely at least one wants to be friends.

The villagers speak of their exiled brethren.
They tell tales of their dark arcanists who
 have a method of cheating death itself.
How can this be?!

Villagers have a fairly sophisticated economy.
They produce goods and barter amongst themselves.
I should learn how to trade with them.

Crossbows pack quite the punch! And with a 
 bit of enchantment, the arrows can whiz
 straight through multiple creatures!
Now I can knock two phantoms out of the sky at once!

I wonder if there is any way to bind a compass
 to a fixed location instead of my bed?

This powdery snow is an absolute death trap!
There must be something that can keep me safe...
Hmmm, maybe the rust is charming after all.

Copper would look so pretty if it didn't rust.
I wonder if I can give it a protective coating?

It's time for a duel! If I can beat the masters
 of the crossbow arts, who have been trained to
 hunt their former neighbors. Then, I should be
 ready to push this crossbow to its limits.
research.arcananovum.description.break_o:
Obsidian, yet another crystal to add to my collection.
It's hardness and explosive resistance are such
 useful properties that I want to gather it in tons.
research.arcananovum.description.break_s:
I have heard horror stores from the depths.
They speak of a night sky in the caves,
 glistening with a strange, but familiar glow.
A substance that feels alive? And harbors 
 more experience than any ore mined or smelted.
They speak of an ancient warden who guards it...

These dungeons have so many great uses, if only
 I could make one of my own at my base.
Why don't I just try to take this one with me?
research.arcananovum.description.cat_sca:
Cats are so cute! It almost makes you
 forget they are vicious predators!
I wonder if any mobs are scared of them?
research.arcananovum.description.catch_f:
Fishing is such a relaxing activity.
I should take the time to learn about the various
 aquatic critters as I catch my new few meals.
research.arcananovum.description.concent:
With every Arcana Item I hold it puts a burden on
 my mind. Growing ever closer to discomfort.
I wonder how much I can handle?
research.arcananovum.description.dimensi:
I have seen ruined portals and heard rumors of
 worlds beyond the Overworld, but I have never
 travelled to one. I wonder what they're like?
research.arcananovum.description.drownin:
I should test the limits of how long I can
 hold my breath while working underwater.
research.arcananovum.description.effect_: I wonder if there's an effect that can boost my health?
I wonder if there's an effect that can blind enemies?
I wonder how dolphins slip through the water so quickly?
I wonder if there's an effect that helps with fire?
I wonder if there's an effect that can boost my jumps?
I wonder if there's a consequence to eating bad food?
I wonder if there's an effect that lets me see better?
I wonder if there's an effect that slowly damages enemies?
I wonder if there's an effect that can slow my fall?
I wonder if there's an effect that can slow enemies?
I wonder if there's an effect that can make me stronger?
I wonder if there's an effect that can boost my speed?
I wonder if there's an effect that can weaken enemies?
research.arcananovum.description.feather:
I keep spraining my ankles doing parkour.
I should find a way to make it hurt less.
research.arcananovum.description.find_sp:
I've heard rumors of old dungeons in caves.
I don't know what they are or what they do.
I suppose it is time for some spelunking.
research.arcananovum.description.fish_it:
I wish I could grab items from a further distance.
Maybe there is some way I haven't thought of...
research.arcananovum.description.fish_mo:
I wish I could pull some mobs closer to me.
Maybe there is already a way to do this...
research.arcananovum.description.honey_c:
Honey is pretty tasty, but some folk-tales
 say it can also be used as medicine to cure
 some degenerative and harmful effects.
I should investigate these claims.
research.arcananovum.description.hunger_:
I have always been spoiled for food.
I wonder what it feels like to be truly hungry.
research.arcananovum.description.infuse_:
My Starlight Forge has the potential to do much
 more than simply forge Arcane items.
The power of the starlight that it harnesses 
 should be able to imbue ordinary equipment with
 much better qualities with the help of Stardust.
research.arcananovum.description.kill_bl:
Blazes have a lot of powerful properties.
Flight, fire immunity, flaming projectiles...
Not to mention their rods have innate alchemical
 properties that lead to many possible reactions.
It would benefit me greatly to collect more rods.
research.arcananovum.description.kill_co:
I want to push my sovereign catalyst further!
Divine Arcana is something I have yet to understand,
 but if I can siphon even a little bit into this
 catalyst then it will become immensely useful.
Perhaps Divine Arcana has some relation to soul
 energy? Maybe that soul fueled monstrosity has
 some relation to a Divine entity?
Maybe I can make a stronger one, that is more
 powerful, to draw out some Divine Essence.
research.arcananovum.description.kill_ev:
Illagers... The outcast villager faction.
Somehow, they are quite skilled in the Arcane.
However, their use of Arcana is twisted and dark.
Perhaps this was the reason for their exile?
Regardless, perhaps there is something I can learn
 from their dark ways. I must visit their Arcanists.
research.arcananovum.description.kill_ma:
Magma Cubes are like Slimes, but with the
 evolutionary advantages of being from the Nether.
However, slime is nothing like magma cream.
Magma cream is smooth and warm, instead of sticky.
I wish to collect some to analyze it further.
research.arcananovum.description.kill_sl:
I've never encountered anything as sticky as slime.
This substance will be of great use in large amounts.
research.arcananovum.description.kill_sq:
Squids have a strange self defense mechanism.
It is quite unnerving and almost blinding.
I wonder if their ink works on other creatures?
research.arcananovum.description.level_1:
I always spend experience almost as fast as
 I can accumulate it, or lose it entirely.
I should try to reach a high level instead.
research.arcananovum.description.milk_cl:
Supposedly, milk is a time-tested cure-all
 for any and all effects, both good and bad.
I can't say that I've tried it yet. It could be useful.
research.arcananovum.description.obtain_:
Amethyst shards have proven to be invaluable.
However, they are very fragile and break apart
 when I try to collect them from their geode.
If I can get my hands on bigger chunks of them,
 the cluster will be capable of harnessing and
 focusing much greater amounts of arcane energy.
Maybe this Silk Touch enchantment can lend a hand?

Crystals are far more than just pretty rocks.
Their ordered structure is perfect for withstanding
 immense energy and even changing and channeling it.
Unfortunately, they take millennia to form naturally, 
 and synthesizing them is beyond my capabilities.
I must find a good source of suitable crystals.

I wish I had a way to repair my equipment.
I have some scrap metal that could be used to 
 patch up my tools and armor but if I try to
 hammer it on, the surface beneath caves in.
I need something sturdy to withstand my hammer.

Nether Stars are truly fascinating! They appear to
 be a micro or even nano crystalline structure that
 is suited specifically for channelling soul energy!
I can think of 10,000 things I can use this for, but
 for now, I should just make something to contain it.

Smelting ores must be done at high temperature.
Furnaces take too long to reach such a high heat.
If I reinforce one, maybe it will smelt faster.

The denser the ice, the more slippery it is.
The denser the ice, the more water it contains.
The denser the ice, the colder it remains.
What is the densest ice that I can obtain?

Experience is a strange substance, it is partly
 ethereal but still has some physicality to it.
Finding a way to contain it would be valuable.

Time. It is such a strange... concept? phenomenon?
Whatever it is, I lose track of it frequently.
I need something to automatically keep it for me.

I believe I found the artifact I am looking for.
Unfortunately, it seems mostly inert on its own.
Given the architecture of the ocean monuments,
 it might be reasonable to assume that this oceanic
 heart needs some type of construct to activate.

Creepers are very unique creatures.
I wonder if their brains are equally unique?

Diamonds are rumored to be the world's most durable
 material, found deep within the caves.
Somehow, I have never laid my hands on one...
 It's time to change that!

The time has come to push my Sovereign Catalyst further.
I need to track down a source of Divine Arcana.
There might be multiple ways to do this...

Some species do not perform live birth, they gestate
 in external eggs until they are ready to be born.
Maybe there is something I can gleam from them.

Villagers are strange folk. They practically worship,
 these green gemstones that they barter and work for.
I should try and get my hands on some...

There is a powerful item whose recipe is lost.
Many have set out in search of the mysterious
 Enchanted Golden Apple, but few ever find one.
I assume they used to be made through a secret
 Arcane recipe that has been lost to time.
I wonder if I can extract some Arcana from one
 of these apples to create something... novel.

This world contains some artifacts displaying
 some impressive properties and craftsmanship.
Perhaps I can find one that can channel Arcana...

Endermen are the only interdimensional travellers
 that I have found, despite evidence of others.
They also manage to do dimension hop without a portal.
I believe their eyes have something to do with it,
 but upon their death, their eyes glaze over and
 can only be used to teleport a short distance.
Maybe I can reawaken their natural abilities?

I don't know what these ancient alchemy books are
 on about, but they keep touting gold-encrusted
 melons as the main ingredient in the elixir of life.
I swear on the stars' light... this better be real.

Most materials I have found exhibit low receptivity
 to enchantment and other forms of arcane infusion.
I need something else... Something malleable, maybe?

I do not believe these so called 'guardians' of
 the ocean monuments are their original builders.
Their name suggests they are simply protectors.
Whoever made the monuments must have a mastery over
 water, through natural or artificial means.
Maybe I can find an artifact that holds the key?

I have heard whispers of buried chambers, designed
 by some unknown entity or collective to test others.
Supposedly, these chambers contain rewards for those
 who are capable enough to defeat their trials.
One such reward is rumored to be an incredibly dense
 material, or maybe even a device of some sort.
I might have a few uses for such a thing.

I haven't been able to learn much of this
 ancient divine artifact, other than that
 it is gifted to those deemed worthy to
 lead others by a divine entity.

Lightning is an amazing natural occurrence!
Unfortunately, it seems to strike randomly during
 intense thunderstorms, which don't happen often.
I might not be able to make lightning occur, but
 perhaps I can at the very least get it where I want.
Lightning leaves a unique taste in the air, it smells
 like old copper. That is a good place to start.

This heavy core is stranger than expected.
It certainly lives up to the name, however
 its weight has some variation to it?
The dark core within seems to have some
 control over its local gravity field.
I believe I can forge this into a powerful weapon.

Enchantment Arcana is a very strange thing.
The Enchantment Table sends some type of Arcana
 into nearby books and pulls it back after the
 knowledge infuses the arcane energy.
This altered arcane energy can produce any one
 of a vast many possible enchantment effects.
The enchantment essence is nebulous until used.
Maybe I can revert the enchantment Arcana back 
 into this nebulous state, and isolate it.

I have found that souls harbor a significant
 amount of power; the energy of life itself.
Soul Sand seems to have a small amount of this
 energy stored within it, and I have found some
 evidence to suggest ancient beings feared an
 entity comprised of this soul-harboring material.
I wish to find this entity, and study its potential.

This ancient debris is certainly something!
Fire-proof, blast-proof, harder than diamond!
Too hard, in fact. I can't even shape it into
 any tools or armors in it's pure state.
Maybe I can alloy it with gold to make it more
 workable, and possibly more receptive to Arcana.

Netherite Alloy has been quite the game-changer.
Its hardness even outclasses diamond!
Theoretically, it should make a phenomenal
 pickaxe, with increased speed and durability.
If I can get enough debris, this is worth a try.

Netherite Alloy has been quite the game-changer.
Its hardness even outclasses diamond!
Theoretically, it should hold a sharper edge.
This is something worth pursuing...

Archeological inquiry has dug up the name 'Ceptyus'
 on more than one occasion, mostly in the Deep Dark.
This being was worshipped as a deity to the
 lost inhabitants of the ancient cities.
Did Ceptyus bring the Sculk? Could Ceptyus BE the Sculk?
Whatever Ceptyus is, those who worshipped it favored
 underground exploration and mining a ton of ore.

'Gaialtus' is a name that often comes up in the
 old tales of the worlds' original creation.
That which carved the rivers from stone...
That which planted every seed and sapling...
Some say that Gaialtus whispers in the wind
 to encourage travellers on their long journey.

I need something that is able to automatically
 generate enough force to move blocks around.
Maybe combining iron and redstone could work?

Crystals are far more than just pretty rocks.
Their ordered structure is perfect for withstanding
 immense energy and even changing and channeling it.
Unfortunately, they take millennia to form naturally, 
 and synthesizing them is beyond my capabilities.
I wonder if the Nether produces crystal formations?

The process of destroying and re-placing blocks
 has proven to be too clumsy to relocate some
 more delicate materials like glass and grass.
Perhaps there is a way to be more careful when
 using my tools to break down delicate blocks.

Notes in the old Stronghold libraries speak of a void
 storm known as 'Tenbrous' that ravaged the End
 with bolts of piercing green lightning.
Somehow, just hearing its name makes me sick to my stomach.
However, none of my more recent sources mention
 anything of Tenbrous, or a storm within the End.
Something interesting of note is the connection between
 Tenbrous and the green color associated with the End Portal
 and the color of Endermen's eyes after their demise.
I wonder what happened to this ancient force of devastation.

Normal arrows are too fragile and shoddy to
 withstand arcane enhancement without any sort
 of reinforcement or arcane priming.
I will find a substance to treat the arrows with.

Water's self-replicating nature makes it very
 difficult to clean up in large quantities.
Maybe something can make this task easier?

Enchantments bind to equipment in a unique way.
Beyond the enchantment Arcana, they alter the
 material they bind to, to a small extent.
There may be a way to isolate and concentrate
 the enhanced material so that this effect can
 be replicated independently of enchantment.
Theoretically, this would look like a powder
 that exhibits some latent arcane properties.

Arrows might be too shoddy to be enhanced by
 Arcana, however, I might be able to coat them
 with some potions to deliver an extra kick.

Creepers are fascinating creatures, with a unique
 capability for self-destructive obliteration.
I would very much like to replicate this power.

I'll keep it real. How am I keeping my arcane 
 notes without a tome to store them in?
This must be rectified immediately!

Enderia, the fabled Empress of The End...
I know little of her, other than she is quite
 possibly one of the oldest beings still alive.
I also have learned that she is a mad tyrant, 
 who rules over the Enderman with a cold heart.
It is possible she also possesses Divine Arcana...

Zombies are very versatile creatures, and
 their possible origins still elude me.
I wonder how similar their brain is to other
 creatures I have encountered in the world.
research.arcananovum.description.place_t:
A stupid creeper blew up my beautiful yard again!
I can't keep letting this happen! I am going to
 light up the entire area around my base!
research.arcananovum.description.resonat:
Villages all have this central bell, and they
 place great value on it, like their lives
 depend on it to protect them or something.
I wonder what is so special about it?
research.arcananovum.description.riptide:
I am trying to figure out ways to propel myself.
I have heard that those with mastery over tridents
 can use them to move through the water quickly.
I should see if I can try this out.
research.arcananovum.description.sprint_:
In this vast world I must travel frequently.
In the interest of getting better at moving around
 I should probably get some better cardio in.
research.arcananovum.description.tame_ca:
Villages keep these adorable creatures with them.
I believe they call them 'cats' and they look
 to be quite powerful allies to have.
I should befriend one of my own.
research.arcananovum.description.unlock_:
This design needs to build on a simpler
 eversource that I have yet to make...

This project requires Arcane infrastructure
 that is capable of complete mastery over
 Arcana infusion with absolute precision.
Without that, there is no way I can make this.

To begin delving into Arcane Augmentation,
 I must first adapt my Runic Matrix into a
 form designed specifically for catalysis.

I might be skipping steps here...
I should understand lesser catalysts first.

I might be skipping steps here...
I should understand lesser catalysts first.

This contraption requires a substance that
 can cause warps in the fabric of space-time.
There is no point in continuing this schematic
 until I have discovered this type of fuel.

This project requires advanced enchantment
 capabilities that I do not have right now.
If I can upgrade my infrastructure a bit, 
 then this project would be more feasible.

I might be skipping steps here...
I should understand lesser catalysts first.

This blueprint is a bit too bold for me now.
I should break this down into smaller steps,
 and design a non-runic quiver first.

This equipment needs a dedicated workstation
 for my archery related inventions.
I will work on that prior to completing this.

This design needs something capable of adapting
 to different forms of Arcana in a modular way.
I think I must invent something else first...

This device relies on a gravimetric core to defy
 gravity itself. However, I currently do not have
 any schematics for such gravity manipulation.
Developing that first would be a better plan.

This schematic draws upon too much soul energy
 to be done with only Nether Stars and soul sand.
I need to design a better soul harness first.

I should maximize the potential of my Catalytic
 Matrix myself before attempting something as
 bold as chasing down Divine Arcana.

This design requires absolute mastery over
 monster spawners. However, I have yet to
 successfully find a way to move them.
That is a more important research endeavor.

This blueprint expands on the infrastructure
 that is used to forge Arcane items.
Right now I have no such infrastructure,
 I will return once I have completed some.

This schematic would require a reinforced
 frame that I do not have the capability
 to create. I should upgrade my forge setup
 and then return once I have better equipment.

This design is based around the ability to
 manipulate time. I am afraid that is beyond
 my current capabilities.
Until I make a new discovery, this is infeasible.

My notes on this are scattered...
This has something to do with transmutation?
I think I need to learn how to transmute things...

This blueprint would require some infrastructure
 to forge Netherite and augment Arcana.
I will come back to this once I am prepared.

I need a way to help mark a location.
Perhaps a stone can encode a static point?
I think I've seen something like this before...
research.arcananovum.description.use_cam:
Cooking food takes fuel that could be used on ores.
Maybe there is alternative cooking method I can try.
research.arcananovum.description.use_enc:
At last I have found an Enchanted Golden Apple!
They say this is the source of some of the most
 potent regeneration Arcana in the world.
I suppose there is only one way to find out...
I wonder what it will taste like?
research.arcananovum.description.use_end:
I have found a new use for Eyes of Ender.
Aside from their natural homing ability that
 I recently discovered, they seem to also peer
 through dimensions, maybe for extra navigation?
However the Endermen warp themselves, they briefly
 enter an interdimensional space, an astral void.
I might be able to use this place for storage.

Interestingly enough, Blaze Powder was the key
 to re-awakening the Enderman's eye.
However, it does not appear to possess the
 enhanced teleportation abilities I expected.
There may be more to this mystery yet.

A poor Enderman got caught out in the rain.
As its flesh melted away from the water, only a
 strange green pearl remained from behind its eyes.
I wonder why it didn't melt. What does it do?
research.arcananovum.description.use_fir:
I wonder if there is a non-destructive use
 for gunpowder? Maybe something festive?
research.arcananovum.description.use_fli:
Fire is such a useful tool. I wish it didn't
 take a whole setup to start one though.
Maybe there is a tool I can make to start fires.
research.arcananovum.description.use_sou:
Surely soul sand doesn't actually contain souls?
If it did, how are they trapped? What do they do?
My curiosity is getting the better of me, I must know.
If it is true, there must be a way to use or free them.
Maybe some of the Nether's inhabitants know...
research.arcananovum.description.visit_d:
The worlds have such unique biodiversity!
I should become acquainted with a handful of
 varying biomes to understand them better.
